def: Under a curse; also used as a mild swear for 'damned'.
Showing good sense and clear thinking.
Extremely good; delightful.
An arrangement that people agree on.
Connected as friends with someone.
Something given in return for good work or help.
The holy book of the Christian religion.
For that reason (informal).
The crime of taking a person away by force.
The ability to form pictures and ideas in the mind.
A large mass of stone.
The sound of people laughing.
Sure and definite; also a certain, unnamed one.
A curved piece for hanging or catching things; also a catch or snag.
A large natural hole in rock or under the ground.
A member of the native peoples of the Americas.
A senior police officer or official.
The goods carried by a ship, truck, or plane; also a summons.
A perfect, beautiful place of happiness.
To take or pull something out (informal).
A noble soldier on horseback in the Middle Ages.
A person who secretly gathers information for another.
An act that breaks a religious or moral law.
To use up something valuable carelessly.
For what reason; because of which.
A woman whose husband has died.
In front of what; also what one is afraid of.
A large, dry area of land with little water or plant life.
Done on purpose.
So unreasonable that it seems ridiculous.
To feel that something is true before knowing for sure.
A false reason given to avoid blame or a duty.
To make someone suffer for doing wrong.
Strange or unusual in a distinctive way.
To be real or present in the world.
Calm and free from conflict or violence.
A person held prisoner to force others to meet demands.
Not able or likely to cause harm.
Able to learn, understand, and think well.
A large, heavy gun that fires shells.
Allowed by the law.
Following clear, sensible reasoning.
Relating to people; also kind and humane.
Ideas about right and wrong behaviour; also team spirit.
Pleasingly small and charming.
Of no use; serving no purpose.
Involving the chance of loss or danger.
Soft and mild; not rough or forceful.
A large farm building for storing crops or keeping animals.
Having no purpose or meaning.
Not fair or just.
Not polite; showing bad manners.
Not needed.
Not able to be seen.
To stop something or someone for a while.
Not important.
Making people think something is wrong.
Deep understanding and good judgement gained through experience.
To hold out against something without giving in.
Extremely good; of very high quality.
The act of giving one thing and getting another in return.
A person who writes books or other texts.
A connection to something; also a cover for a cushion.
A clear difference between two things.
The place in the ground where a dead person is buried.
A person admired for brave or great deeds.
A hard effort against an opponent or difficulty.
A man or boy (informal).
A coal miner; also an informal word for a close friend.
A government department led by a minister.
An informal word for grandfather.
The son of a king or queen.
A period of three months in a business year.
Silly or foolish talk (informal).
A loss of respect caused by shameful behaviour.
A weapon with a long metal blade and a handle.
The act of deliberately ending one's own life.
A short piece of modern popular music with words.
The sandy or stony shore beside the sea.
A feeling that someone has done wrong, without proof.
The state of being insane; also something crazy or amazing.
An event that happens without being planned.
Complete disorder and confusion.
Mud, dust, or filth (informal).
A large round container for liquids like beer or wine.
The work of looking after someone ill or old; also upkeep.
A soft cloth with a thick, smooth surface.
The result of cutting; also an average or a style of cutting.
A sign, light, or sound that gives information or a warning.
A sharp prick or bite; also a stitch in sewing.
A series of movements made to music.
A clever act done to fool or amuse people.
A distance ahead of others; also a part that sticks out.
Happening in turn, one after the other.
Ready to attack or act forcefully.
A firm call for someone to do something.
The branch of engineering dealing with electricity.
The flat heated part of a cooker where pans are placed.
Introducing new ideas or methods.
A person who is interested in buying or taking part.
A small case holding tape for recording sound.
A famous international prize for outstanding achievement.
A person who plans and arranges an event.
A machine that plays music from vinyl records.
Rubbish that cannot be recycled.
A flat vinyl disc that stores and plays music.
A soft, fine cloth made from the threads of silkworms.
A light shirt worn next to the skin under other clothes.
A treatment using thin needles placed in the body.
The Christian day that begins the fasting period of Lent.
Extra force given to a word or point.
A celebration giving thanks for the year's crops.
To hold or stare at something steadily; to fix in place.
A period of work that starts early in the morning.
A tight, pushing crowd of people.
Relating to skilled work done by hand.
The doorway leading into a building.
Bright and happy; also, of weather, fair.
A small device worn in the ear to help someone hear.
Beautifully peaceful and charming.
Relating to industry and factories.
Allowing two-way action between a user and a system.
An overused idea or phrase that has lost its force.
Tiny bits of coloured paper thrown at celebrations.
The keeping of goods in a place until they are needed.
Relaxed and informal in style.
A house divided into homes for several families.
A race in which horses are ridden in competition.
Hair tied together and hanging down at the back.
A condition causing pain and stiffness in the joints.
A shout or cry; also the opinion people have of someone.
Travel or transport of goods by ship.
A period of work that runs into the evening.
A knitted jacket that fastens at the front.
To stand for or represent something.
A very young baby.
A piece of business, such as buying or paying.
To sell something to the highest bidder.
Relating to seeing or sight.
The moon when it appears as a complete bright circle.
A film or story set in the American Old West.
Broken into pieces; shattered.
The act of putting something into a group or matching it.
The imaginary line around the middle of the earth.
The close watching of a person or place.
